people.text: Harper wants beginner-friendly fitness sessions with patient teachers and clear instructions.
Logan enjoys social evening activities connected with fitness and meeting new people.
Quinn prefers calm organised groups with step-by-step guidance and small classes.
Parker needs inexpensive activities near buses or trains because they travel after work.
Reese likes creative projects and wants personal feedback from experienced instructors.

texts.text: Classes are [8]carefully organised[/8] and limited to small groups. Leaders provide [8]step-by-step guidance[/8] for nervous newcomers.
Experienced teachers provide [10]individual feedback[/10] and encourage participants to develop [10]creative personal ideas[/10].
This course teaches [6]basic fitness skills[/6] through practical activities. Teachers give [6]clear beginner support[/6] and answer questions carefully throughout the session.
The venue offers [9]low-cost sessions[/9] close to the central bus and train stations, making it useful for [9]people travelling after work[/9].
Weekend family sessions are designed mainly for young children and parents.
This intensive programme prepares experienced participants for competitions and public events.
Advanced users prepare independently and complete additional practice at home every week.
Members meet [7]on weekday evenings[/7] and spend time talking before activities begin. The atmosphere is relaxed and suitable for people interested in [7]meeting others socially[/7].

answers.explanation: Harper needs beginner-friendly support and clear explanations.
Parker needs cheap activities near transport.
Reese wants creative work with personal feedback.
